NORTHROOT
ProductSystemsDocsJournalExamples

Northroot trust kernel

Portable evidence for verifiable work.

Northroot is a trust kernel for append-only records, receipts, and offline verification across operational and agentic work. Runtime systems can build around it without becoming the source of proof.

Core append-only records, receipts, portable journals
Checks offline verification with explicit outcomes
Boundary kernel first; runtime and console remain separate

Capabilities

Core operations

PrimitivePurpose
Canonical JSONStable bytes for the same semantic value.
RecordsAppend-oriented evidence for what happened.
ReceiptsClaims bound to local evidence hashes.
JournalsOptional framed replay for offline audit.
VerificationChecks that do not require a live service.

Start here

Read the current system

Portable Evidence defines the current proof shape.

Product describes the kernel scope and current non-goals.

Systems explains publishing boundaries and source authority.

Architecture

One evidence shape across runtimes

Different systems can produce records and receipts that remain inspectable outside the system that produced them.

RecordsStructured evidence from external systems.
CanonicalizeDeterministic bytes and hygiene rules.
ReceiptClaim bound to evidence hashes.
VerifyOffline check with no live service dependency.

Examples

First portable bundle

Start with the GitHub repo inspection example to see a read-only agentic work item recorded as files, hashes, events, and a receipt.

Open example

Publishing

Drafts require review

draft PR gated

Generated drafts carry source references and remain unpublished until reviewed and merged.

Review the boundary
